markdown.gemini:
# Al-Azhar Mosque (جامع الأزهر)
**Status:** PUBLISHED_MEDIA  
**District:** Al-Darb al-Ahmar / Al-Azhar  
**Type:** Mosque & University  

## Historical Summary
Founded in 970 AD by the Fatimid general Jawhar al-Siqilli shortly after the conquest of Egypt. It grew to become one of the premier centers of Sunni Islamic learning globally.

*تأسس عام 970 م على يد القائد الفاطمي جوهر الصقلي عقب فتح مصر، ليتحول لاحقاً إلى أحد أهم مراكز العلوم الإسلامية في العالم.*

## Architectural Details
A composite complex illustrating multi-era Islamic architecture, featuring Fatimid keel-arch arcades, Mamluk minarets (such as Qaytbay and al-Ghuri), and Ottoman expansions.

### Key Elements
- Fatimid Stucco
- Multiple Minarets
- Marble-Paved Courtyard
- Mamluk Porticos

## Stories & Heritage Significance
### Foundation of Cairo (تأسيس القاهرة)
**Classification:** fact
Al-Azhar was the first congregational mosque built in Fatimid Al-Qahira.

## Visitor Information
- **Opening Hours:** 05:00 - 22:00 (Outside prayer times preferred for sightseers)
- **Ticket Price:** Free entry
- **Photography Rules:** Non-disruptive photography permitted in courtyard
- **Dress Code:** Strict modest dress; shoes must be removed or covered
- **Accessibility:** Courtyard accessible; ramp available at side entrance

## Photo Candidates
- **[COVER]** Al-Azhar Courtyard and Minarets (CC BY 4.0)
  - Direct Link: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/1/12/Al-Azhar_Mosque_Courtyard.jpg
  - Attribution: CC BY 4.0 via Wikimedia Commons

## Sources
- **[SRC-ARCHNET-AZHAR]** [Archnet: Al-Azhar Mosque](https://archnet.org/sites/1512) - *Aga Khan Documentation Center at MIT*
- **[SRC-MTA-AZHAR]** [Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities - Al-Azhar Mosque](https://egymonuments.gov.eg/en/monuments/al-azhar-mosque) - *Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities*

## Unresolved Items
No open issues.
